message:
fix(cx): cancellation evidence, mask depth hardening, macaroon trailing bytes, registry waiter grant

cx.rs:
- Emit cancellation evidence at checkpoint when cancel is requested and mask
  depth is zero, providing observability into cancellation decisions via the
  evidence_sink infrastructure
- Remove dead `check_cancel()` method that duplicated checkpoint logic without
  recording the checkpoint itself — all callers should use checkpoint() which
  combines both concerns
- Upgrade mask depth overflow guard from debug_assert + error log to a single
  hard assert: the debug_assert was only checked in debug builds while the error
  log ran in release but allowed silent continuation past INV-MASK-BOUNDED
  violation. A hard assert is correct because exceeding MAX_MASK_DEPTH means
  cancellation can never be observed, which is a program logic error

macaroon.rs:
- Reject trailing bytes after the signature in token deserialization — a
  well-formed token must be exactly `pos` bytes, and accepting trailing data
  could mask serialization bugs or allow trivial token confusion attacks
- Add debug_assert on xor_pad slice lengths to catch mismatched caveat key
  encryption inputs early in development

registry.rs:
- cancel_permit() now takes a `now: Time` parameter and calls
  try_grant_to_first_waiter() when a pending name is cancelled, so queued
  waiters are promptly notified instead of waiting for the next register attempt
- Deduplicate lease iteration in unregister_task: reuse the already-collected
  active_removed vec instead of scanning leases a second time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
